title: AVP Data Product Manager
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 This role is focused on forging robust partnerships with key stakeholders to understand their data requirements and priorities across various lines of business.  Responsible for identifying, assessing, documenting, planning, and managing data solutions which enhance the Bank’s business intelligence and analytical capabilities.    
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  
+ Identifies, documents, and defines reporting and analytical solutions with business stakeholders to strategically improve their consumption and utilization of business data.
  
+ Partners with the Data Innovation Team architects and engineers to convey requirements to support data and report development activities.
  
+ Creates logical descriptive solution drawings, process flows, wireframes, and other supporting collateral to define the solution.
  
+ Supports creation of testing plans and test templates to support successful implementation of the solution.
  
+ Provides release planning coordination across data products.
  
+ Anticipates and mitigates roadblocks that impact the team’s ability to complete release objectives.
  
+ Prepares and updates data documentation and limited training material to insure successful feature implementation and adoption.
  
+ Builds an understanding of assigned lines of business.
  
+ Utilizes a proactive, professional, and team-oriented style to work effectively with executive leaders, department heads, and subject matter experts.
  
+ Assists in project planning activities including creation of deliverables, milestones, timelines, project assignments, and constraints/dependencies management.
  
+ Identifies, communicates, and mitigates risks and opportunities, and contributes to process improvements.
  
+ Enables the data development lifecycle and adheres to the Bank’s development controls.
  
+ Maintains awareness of business intelligence and analytics landscape to ensure leading edge capabilities and practices are utilized within the Bank.
  
+ Provides post-development support by leading communication, outreach, and training, and by removing impediments that prevent releases, rollouts, and user adoption.
  
+ Promotes and maintains a positive work atmosphere by demonstrating leadership and communicating in a positive, professional manner to work effectively with co-workers, management, partners, and vendors.
  
+ Regularly exercises discretion and judgment in the performance of essential job functions.
  
+ Maintains good punctuality and attendance to work.
  
+ Follows Bank policy, procedures and guidelines. 
  

  

  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  
+ Knowledge of Jira or similar task management platform.
  
+ Knowledge of Microsoft BI stack including SQL Server Database, SSIS, SSAS, Power BI, SQL Reporting Services.
  
+ Knowledge of cloud architecture including but not limited to Microsoft Azure.
  
+ Ability to communicate effectively through verbal and written methods.
  
+ Ability to speak clearly and persuasively in positive and negative situations while emphasizing teamwork and team-based outcomes.
  
+ Ability to develop and grow organizational knowledge.
  
+ Ability to work on multiple projects at once, multi-task, with exacting deadlines.
  
+ Ability to demonstrate accuracy, thoroughness. and attention to detail.
  
+ Ability to exhibit sound and accurate judgment.
  
+ Ability to work alone or in group settings.
  
+ Ability to demonstrate critical thinking, analytical skills, and organization.
  
+ Ability to work effectively with all levels of management.
  
+ Ability to demonstrate effective project management and customer service skills.
  
+ Skill in using computer, including Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint and Excel). 
  

  

  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  
+ Bachelor’s degree or commensurate work experience required.
  
+ Minimum of four (4) years of work experience in a data analyst, data science, or data engineering role, including direct experience in technical product management and/or data analysis activities, required.
  
+ Prior work experience in data product management preferred. 
  

  

  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation. 
  

  
 
  

  
 Note: This description is not an exhaustive list of all job functions, duties, skills, and job standards required.  Other job functions, duties, skills, and standards may be added.  Management reserves the right to add or change the job requirements at any time.

title: Loan Officer - Vacation Relief, AACU Branch Operations
description_text:
**Intro**
  

  
Are you ready to explore a world of possibilities, both at work and during your time off? Join our American Airlines family, and you’ll travel the world, grow your expertise and become the best version of you. As you embark on a new journey, you’ll tackle challenges with flexibility and grace, learning new skills and advancing your career while having the time of your life. Feel free to enrich both your personal and work life and hop on board!
  

  
**Why you'll love this job**
  

  
The American Airlines Credit Union is seeking a bilingual, adaptable  **Loan Officer**  dedicated to building long‑term member relationships by aligning member needs with Credit Union products, services, and lending solutions.
  

  
**What you'll do**
  

  
+ Provide coverage to AACU branches in and out of state. This means traveling with little to no notice and potential overnights.
  
+ ​Process new loan applications, which includes interviewing the member, analyzing credit reports and documentation and offering the best product to meet member needs
  
+ Exercise lending authority to correctly handle all loan requests, including but not limited to consumer loans
  
+ Create and promote positive name recognition for the Credit Union
  
+ Support or speak publicly at special Credit Union events
  
+ Work with branch/department managers and staff to identify and leverage cross-selling opportunities
  
+ Research member inquiries and provide solutions as required in a timely manner
  
+ Maintain awareness of all branch functions and duties to ensure proper support and coverage is provided at all times
  
+ Implement and enforce internal control and security measures in accordance with Credit Union policies and government regulations
  
+ Foster a culture that promotes teamwork, service excellence and relationship building
  
+ Handle responsibilities of a Member Services Representative as needed
  
+ Perform Notary Services as required
  
+ Travel to various branch locations nationally to provide coverage
  
+ May work a rotating Saturday schedule depending on job location
  
+ Perform other duties as assigned
  
+ The selected candidate will be responsible for ensuring the security and confidentiality of all account and related information which is part of their work and for ensuring that his/her work is in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations including, but not limited to, the Bank Secrecy Act.
  

  
**All you'll need for success**
  

  
**Minimum Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ ​High school diploma or GED
  
+ Must be able to work in-person at assigned branch location
  
+ Must have strong working background in a financial institution and relevant consumer loan processing or underwriting experience within the last twelve months
  
+ Job level to be determined by the candidate's skills, qualifications and experience
  
+ Must be bilingual in Spanish (read, speak and write)
  
+ Comfortable with traveling with little to no notice to cover AACU branches in and out of state.
  

  
**Preferred Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, related area, or equivalent financial services experience, or a combination of education and experience that provides the necessary skills and knowledge to satisfactorily perform the essential job functions
  
+ Lending and sales-culture envronment experience
  

  
**Skills, Licenses & Certifications**
  

  
+ ​Skilled in Microsoft Office software (e.g., Word, Excel, Access, PowerPoint) gained through either work experience with the software or education and hands-on use of the software
  
+ Must fulfill FAA criminal background checks to qualify for unescorted access privileges to airport security identification display areas (SIDA), if applicable
  
+ Ability to exercise excellent member service skills
  
+ Ability to perform in a sales-culture environment with a desire to cross sell Credit Union products and services
  
+ Ability to perform in a fast paced environment, handle multiple tasks and function as an integral part of a team
  
+ Ability to interact professionally with all employees and members
  
+ Ability to speak, read, and write in English is required; Spanish is a plus
  
+ Ability to work independently and with minimal supervision
  
+ Ability to perform basic math calculations and analyze data
  
+ Ability to self-motivate with strong organization skills and capacity for attention to detail
  
+ Ability to effectively prioritize work and meet deadlines
  
+ Ability to sit and/or stand for extended periods of time
  
+ Ability to work varying schedules, including evenings and weekends
  
+ Ability to travel overnight
  
+ Ability to maintain satisfactory performance and attendance
  
+ Ability to pass applicable American Airlines and Credit Union pre-hire compliance checks.
  

  
**What you'll get**
  

  
Feel free to take advantage of all that American Airlines has to offer:
  

  
+ Travel Perks: Ready to explore the world? You, your family and your friends can reach 365 destinations on more than 6,800 daily flights across our global network.
  
+ Health Benefits: On day one, you’ll have access to your health, dental, prescription and vision benefits to help you stay well. And that’s just the start, we also offer virtual doctor visits, flexible spending accounts and more.
  
+ Wellness Programs: We want you to be the best version of yourself – that’s why our wellness programs provide you with all the right tools, resources and support you need.
  
+ 401(k) Program: Available upon hire and, depending on the workgroup, employer contributions to your 401(k) program are available after one year.
  
+ Additional Benefits: Other great benefits include our Employee Assistance Program, pet insurance and discounts on hotels, cars, cruises and more
  

  
**Feel free to be yourself at American**
  

  
From the team members we hire to the customers we serve, inclusion and diversity are the foundation of the dynamic workforce at American Airlines. Our 20+ Employee Business Resource Groups are focused on connecting our team members to our customers, suppliers, communities and shareholders, helping team members reach their full potential and creating an inclusive work environment to meet and exceed the needs of our diverse world.
  

  
Are you ready to feel a tremendous sense of pride and satisfaction as you do your part to keep the largest airline in the world running smoothly as we care for people on life’s journey? Feel free to be yourself at American.

title: Project Manager, Variable Team
description_text:
**About the Role:**
  
As a CBRE Project Management Consultant, you will be responsible for providing consulting services to an assigned market or client account to help achieve the company's strategic business objectives.
  

  
This job is part of the Project Management function. They are responsible for the management of projects from initiation through completion.
  

  
**What You’ll Do:**
  

  
+ Manage all areas of project management for singular real estate projects. This includes planning, design, construction, occupancy, and closeout.
  
+ Work directly with clients to prepare all elements of the project. This includes the scope of work, project delivery resource requirements, cost estimates & budget, work plan schedule & milestones, quality control, and risk identification.
  
+ Define the project delivery resources from pre-qualified lists or through the project qualification process. Conduct a standard request for proposals, complete bid evaluations, and recommend resources to clients.
  
+ Implement project documentation governance that is aligned with company and client requirements.
  
+ Ensure project data integrity and documentation is accurate, timely, and coordinated.
  
+ Direct the project delivery team by providing guidance and direction to achieve goals.
  
+ Create action plans to meet objectives, budget, and schedule. Track the progress of each project and report status and variances.
  
+ Demonstrate ability to identify project threats and develop risk mitigation and contingency plans. Implement action plans to reduce or eliminate project risks.
  
+ Apply in-depth knowledge of standard principles and techniques/procedures to accomplish complex assignments and provide innovative solutions.
  
+ Coach others and share in-depth knowledge of own job discipline and broad knowledge of several job disciplines within the function.
  
+ Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values. Work to build consensus and convince others to reach an agreement.
  
+ Impact a range of customer, operational, project, or service activities within own team and other related teams.
  
+ Work within broad guidelines and policies.
  
+ Explain difficult or sensitive information.
  

  
+ Bachelor's Degree preferred with 5-8 years of relevant experience. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered. LEED AP, PMP, and/or CCM designation(s) preferred.
  
+ Ability to exercise judgment based on the analysis of multiple sources of information.
  
+ Willingness to take a new perspective on existing solutions.
  
+ In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
+ Organizational skills with an advanced inquisitive mindset.
  
+ Sophisticated math skills. Ability to calculate mildly complex figures such as percentages, fractions, and other financial-related calculations.
  

  
**What You’ll Need:**
  
Turner & Townsend carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. The minimum salary for this position is $120,000 annually and the maximum salary for this position is $140,000 annually. The compensation that is offered to a successful candidate will depend on the candidate’s skills, qualifications, and experience.  Successful candidates will also be eligible for a discretionary bonus based on Turner & Townsend’s applicable benefit program. This role will provide the following benefits: 401(K), Dental insurance, Health insurance, Life insurance, and Vision insurance Disclaimer: Please be advised that effective January 1, 2025, CBRE Project Management and Turner &Townsend were consolidated into a single global business entity. As a candidate applying for a position, you should be aware that while your initial employment may be with CBRE, you will subsequently transfer directly to Turner & Townsend at a date to be determined.
  

  
Equal Employment Opportunity: CBRE is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We have a long-standing commitment to providing equal employment opportunity to all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status, political belief, or any other basis protected by applicable law.
  

  
Candidate Accommodations: CBRE values the differences of all current and prospective employees and recognizes how every employee contributes to our company’s success. CBRE provides reasonable accommodations in job application procedures for individuals with disabilities. title: Program Management Director - Great Lakes Region
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
The Program Management Director for Great Lakes Region is responsible for the managerial leadership and direction of large, complex program execution within the Great Lakes Region (GLR). This role will align with the Global Program Management Organization (GPMO) strategy and planning, with a focus on delivering results for Program development, and execution. The Regional Director of Program Management will develop and lead a high-performing team of program managers and support personnel, driving successful program execution and fostering a culture of collaboration and excellence. This role is pivotal in developing and training top talent within the region's program execution space.
  
 
  

  
 
  
The Regional Director will be responsible for leading the GLR program management team in a dynamic and profitable manner, based on Burns & McDonnell's core values of client satisfaction, commitment, dedication, integrity, safety and overall service. The role will cover multiple global practices with emphasis on program management growth in new markets.
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Lead the development and execution of the GLR program's strategic business initiatives to achieve regional and global goals and objectives.
  
 
  
+ Provide direction and support for GLR business development efforts in the identification and vetting of prospective clients and programs.
  
 
  
+ Provide leadership to the GLR Program team; direct and coordinate the multi-discipline department, maximizing efficient and cost-effective utilization of staff.
  
 
  
+ Evaluate the health of major programs within the GLR, identify and mitigate risks, analyze deficiencies and implement changes as required.
  
 
  
+ Oversee the development and implementation of department tools and workflows to increase efficiency in GLR program execution.
  
 
  
+ Collaborate with the Global Program Management Organization (GPMO) and other regional leaders to maintain alignment and consistent application of the program management model.
  
 
  

  
 
  

  
* Liaise with company stakeholders and provide strategic leadership to progress the Program Management Office model nationally. Collaborate cross-functionally to align strategies that drive client engagement, operational excellence, and market growth across industries and geographies, ensuring consistent and high-quality implementation of the program management model enterprise-wide.
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Direct oversight and management of GLR program budgets and expenditure of funds.
  
 
  
+ Achieve stated targets and standards for the GLR's program financial performance.
  
 
  
+ Present monthly GLR financial information to leadership, including program revenue and sales forecasts.
  
 
  
+ Support strategies for cross-selling engineering, consulting and other services across the organization.
  
 
  
+ Contribute to the development of long-term strategies for GLR program growth and service diversification.
  
 
  
+ Recruit, hire, develop and retain program management employee-owners within the GLR.
  
 
  
+ Coordinate with other GLR global practices to align existing program management staff with broader GLR business plans and organizational structures.
  
 
  
+ Responsible for mentoring, managing, training and conducting performance evaluations for direct reports.
  
 
  
+ Implement, and maintain compliance with Burns & McDonnell's Quality Control program, as well as company and site safety policies.
  
 
  
+ Build and maintain strong client relationships, ensuring that collaborative efforts are aligned with client success.
  
 
  
+ Target new markets and clients that have program management opportunities through proposal development, client presentations, and business development
  
 
  
+ Lead risk reviews for new opportunities in collaboration with key stakeholders from GLR and GPMO.
  
 
  
+ Foster a culture of innovation by encouraging new ideas and solutions to complex challenges.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor Degree in engineering, business or related degree from an accredited program. and 13 years relevant consulting experience, preferably in the engineering or construction industry or project management required.
  
 
  
+ Expert experience in client and business development.
  
 
  
+ 5–10 years of progressive experience in strategic planning, change management, talent development, organizational transformation, and operational excellence, including building, scaling, or transforming organizations in leadership, strategy, or organizational development roles preferred.
  
 
  
+ Data-driven decision-making skills - substantial experience using metrics, KPIs, and analytics to assess organizational health and performance.
  
 
  
+ Advanced certifications such as EVMP, PMP, PMI-CP or PSP are highly preferred.
  
 
  
+ Expert experience in client and business development, with a proven track record of winning and managing large-scale programs.
  
 
  
+ Diverse and extensive industry experience, with a preference for experience in industrial, utilities, commercial, or any other complex construction related environment. Target industries are power, transmission & distribution, buildings & infrastructure, aviation, federal, water, and environmental.
  
 
  
+ Expert experience in managing programs, including proposal development, contract negotiations, program planning, design team coordination, communication, budgeting and schedule management.
  
 
  
+ Expert facilitation, collaboration, organization and problem-solving skills, with a demonstrated ability to thoughtfully and positively influence, lead and manage.
  
 
  
+ Proven success in relation to budget, savings and/or profit margins on completed programs.
  
 
  
+ Excellent planning, analytical and communication skills, with the ability to present complex information to all levels of employees, management and clients.
  
 
  
+ Expert knowledge of program management methodologies and tools, such as Procore, Oracle Primavera P6, or other industry standard PMIS tools.
  
 
  
+ Willingness to travel up to 50% to program and company locations as required.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Compensation**
  
 
  

  
 
  
$205,000.00-450,000.00 Yearly
  
 
  
The expected compensation range for this position is displayed in compliance with all local/state regulations. The expected compensation range for this position is based on a number of factors, including but not limited to: individual education, qualifications, prior work experience and work location. The total annual compensation package will consist of a base salary and eligibility to participate in our discretionary year-end incentive bonus program.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Benefits**
  
 
  

  
 
  
Our extensive benefits package takes care of you so that you can focus on doing great work. From insurance and disability to time off and wellness programs, we provide the tools to meet your needs. As part of being 100% employee-owned, eligible employees participate in our Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) in addition to our 401(k) retirement program. For more information, please visit the Benefits & Wellness page.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.

title: SVP, Investigations and Recovery
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 Responsible for leading the Fraud Investigations and Recovery function of the Bank's Fraud Risk Management Program, including overall accountability for the performance of fraud investigations, loss recovery, and case oversight functions. Provides leadership and accountability for the team responsible for investigating fraud incidents, pursuing loss recovery, managing case oversight, and advancing program development. Establishes the standards, priorities, and controls that ensure investigations are handled accurately, timely, and in accordance with departmental standards, regulatory expectations, and Bank policy. 
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  

  

  

  
+  Leads the Fraud Investigations and Recovery function of the Bank, holding overall accountability for the performance of investigations, recovery, and case oversight functions. 
  

  
+  Manages leaders and staff, to include staffing, assigning and delegating work, training, mentoring, and coaching. Conducts performance reviews and fosters a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. 
  

  
+  Provides oversight of the daily work of the Fraud Investigations and Recovery team, including queue management, case assignments, investigative support, escalation handling, and workload balancing, directly to team members and through subordinate managers. 
  

  
+  Serves as the senior escalation point for the most complex, high-risk, or reputationally sensitive fraud cases, recovery matters, and investigative findings. 
  

  
+  Partners with internal and external stakeholders to mitigate fraud loss and strengthen the Bank's overall Fraud Risk Management Program. 
  

  
+  Establishes and enforces standards to ensure fraud investigations are completed timely and accurately and that case documentation, referrals, and supporting materials meet departmental, regulatory, and audit expectations. 
  

  
+  Directs recovery efforts related to fraud incidents by coordinating with internal stakeholders, external partners, and law enforcement to pursue loss mitigation and recovery opportunities. 
  

  
+  Oversees the identification, investigation, and reporting of elder financial exploitation to the appropriate internal groups and external agencies. 
  

  
+  Collaborates with Fraud Prevention, Fraud Strategy, frontline employees, and other lines of business to identify fraud trends, strengthen controls, and address control gaps. 
  

  
+  Advises senior management on fraud-related incidents, case trends, emerging threats, financial fraud typologies, and program performance. 
  

  
+  Develops and maintains professional working relationships with local and federal law enforcement, regulatory agencies, and industry peers to support investigations, recovery, and awareness of emerging fraud trends. 
  

  
+  Directs the development, enhancement, and documentation of investigations and recovery processes, procedures, job aids, and controls to promote an effective and scalable program. 
  

  
+  Represents Fraud Investigations and Recovery in internal and external audits, regulatory reviews, and rating agency requests, and leads timely responses to findings and remediation activities. 
  

  
+  Serves as a senior subject matter expert on fraud investigations, fraud recovery, and related regulations, policies, and industry best practices. Represents the Bank in industry forums and networks with peer institutions to gain competitive insights and share best practices. 
  

  
+  Provides executive-level reports, trend analyses, and program updates to management, committees, and other interested parties. 
  

  
+  Performs or assigns other tasks as directed by management. 
  

  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  

  

  

  
+  Comprehensive knowledge of banking laws and regulations and related compliance reporting, with the ability to interpret and apply them at a program level. 
  

  
+  Comprehensive knowledge of fraud investigations, investigative techniques, and fraud recovery practices. 
  

  
+  Ability to communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing, including the ability to present case facts, trends, program performance, and recommendations to senior management. 
  

  
+  Ability to utilize discretion and sound judgment in decision-making and maintain confidentiality. 
  

  
+  Ability to read and analyze data for suspicious behavior and investigative follow-up. 
  

  
+  Ability to multi-task, prioritize work, manage multiple projects, follow up on tasks, and meet exacting deadlines. 
  

  
+  Ability to work effectively both in a team environment and independently with little to no supervision. 
  

  
+  Ability to communicate effectively with all levels of management and staff. 
  

  
+  Ability to maintain attention to detail and demonstrate strong organizational, time management, and project management skills. 
  

  
+  Ability to work effectively and demonstrate flexibility in a continually changing environment and deal with frequent change, delays, and unexpected events. 
  

  
+  Ability to work extended hours to accomplish work goals and objectives. 
  

  
+  Ability to travel on company business, including occasional overnight travel or extended trips, both throughout and outside of the company’s footprint. 
  

  
+  Skill in using computer, including advanced proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook 
  

  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  

  

  

  
+  Bachelor's degree in Risk Management, Business, Finance, Criminal Justice, or a related field, or commensurate work experience, required. 
  

  
+  7+ years of work experience in risk management, fraud investigations and/or fraud prevention, required. 
  

  
+  5+ years of financial institution work experience, required. 
  

  
+  4+ years of supervisory or management experience, required. 
  

  

  
   
  

  
 Preferred Qualifications: 
  

  

  
+  Professional certification in a relevant discipline, such as Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE), Certified Financial Crimes Investigator (CFCI), or Certified Anti-Fraud Specialist (CAFS), or willingness to obtain, preferred. 
  

  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation. 
  

  
 
  

  
 Note: This description is not an exhaustive list of all job functions, duties, skills, and job standards required.  Other job functions, duties, skills, and standards may be added.  Management reserves the right to add or change the job requirements at any time.

title: Analyst/Sr Analyst, Business Readiness and Change Management
description_text:
**Intro**
  

  
Are you ready to explore a world of possibilities, both at work and during your time off? Join our American Airlines family, and you’ll travel the world, grow your expertise and become the best version of you. As you embark on a new journey, you’ll tackle challenges with flexibility and grace, learning new skills and advancing your career while having the time of your life. Feel free to enrich both your personal and work life and hop on board!
  

  
**Why you'll love this job**
  

  
•    This role supports the Next Gen Ops Program team in the IOC by turning program goals into adoption plans, coordinating with stakeholders, and ensuring large-scale readiness as legacy systems are replaced. Responsibilities include driving behavioral, process, and role changes for a safe, compliant, and efficient transition in Flight Operations.
  
•    The work is critical for shaping American Airlines’ future capabilities, enabling the organization to adapt swiftly to industry changes, enhance operational efficiency, and maintain a competitive edge in an evolving aviation landscape.
  

  
**What you'll do**
  

  
_As noted above, this list is intended to reflect the current job but there may be additional essential functions (and certainly non-essential job functions) that are not referenced.  Management will modify the job or require other tasks be performed whenever it is deemed appropriate to do so, observing, of course, any legal obligations including any collective bargaining obligations._
  

  
•    Support the change strategy for the Next Gen Ops Program, embedding adoption, readiness, and communications across all phases of transformation.
  
•    Support design and execution of the communications plan—right message, right audience, right channel—to drive awareness, understanding, and engagement.
  
•    Conduct impact assessments and change readiness analyses to identify affected roles, processes, and systems; translate insights into actionable plans.
  
•    Partner with Regulatory & Compliance and Safety to ensure change deliverables align to regulatory requirements and support certification and audit needs tied to the system-of-record transition.
  
•    Coach sponsors, leaders, and managers to model change, reinforce new ways of working, and sustain adoption.
  
•    Partner cross-functionally across Flight Ops, Dispatch, Crew Scheduling, Inflight, Tech Ops, and Technology to synchronize cutovers and readiness milestones.
  
•    Identify, analyze, and mitigate change risks, managing anticipated resistance and escalating issues with clear options and impacts.
  
•    Integrate OCM into program delivery, ensuring readiness checkpoints, training development, and stakeholder engagement are built into release plans.
  
•    Measure and report adoption outcomes (e.g., awareness, engagement, training completion, time-to-proficiency, utilization, error/defect rates, operational KPIs) and adjust tactics based on data.
  
•    Prepare and deliver executive-ready updates on change health, stakeholder sentiment, and readiness status for governance forums.
  
•    Champion a culture of change and continuous improvement, capturing feedback loops and embedding lessons learned into future releases.
  

  
**All you'll need for success**
  

  
**Minimum Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ •    Bachelor’s degree in Business, Communications, Organizational Development, or related field (or equivalent experience)•    3 years as a change advocate (e.g., OCM Specialist, Communications Specialist, Business Analyst, or Product Owner) on technology-enabled programs•    Experience applying structured change management methodologies on medium to large initiatives
  

  
**Preferred Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ •    5+ years in organizational change management for complex, regulated, or safety-critical environments•    Experience with large-scale system implementations or operational transformations (preferably in airline operations or similar)•    Prosci® certification (ADKAR) or equivalent•    Exposure to commercial off‑the‑shelf (COTS) or SaaS solution delivery and enterprise rollouts
  

  
**Skills, Licenses & Certifications**
  

  
+ •    OCM expertise: impact analysis, stakeholder mapping, readiness assessments, reinforcement planning•    Communications excellence: concise storytelling, tailored messaging, and executive communications•    Training & enablement: partner with SMEs/learning teams to scope curricula, measure proficiency, and enable role-based adoption•    Collaboration & influence: build trust across matrixed teams; coach leaders and managers to sponsor and sustain change•    Analytical & outcomes-driven: define adoption KPIs; use data to guide decisions and adapt tactics•    Change agility: resilient, adaptable, and effective in ambiguous, fast-paced environments•    Tools: Proficiency with Microsoft 365 (PowerPoint, Excel, Word, Visio, Project), SharePoint; familiarity with work management tools (e.g., Azure DevOps/JIRA) a plus
  

  
**What you'll get**
  

  
Feel free to take advantage of all that American Airlines has to offer:
  

  
+ Travel Perks: Ready to explore the world? You, your family and your friends can reach 365 destinations on more than 6,800 daily flights across our global network.
  
+ Health Benefits: On day one, you’ll have access to your health, dental, prescription and vision benefits to help you stay well. And that’s just the start, we also offer virtual doctor visits, flexible spending accounts and more.
  
+ Wellness Programs: We want you to be the best version of yourself – that’s why our wellness programs provide you with all the right tools, resources and support you need.
  
+ 401(k) Program: Available upon hire and, depending on the workgroup, employer contributions to your 401(k) program are available after one year.
  
+ Additional Benefits: Other great benefits include our Employee Assistance Program, pet insurance and discounts on hotels, cars, cruises and more
  

  
**Feel free to be yourself at American**
  

  
From the team members we hire to the customers we serve, inclusion and diversity are the foundation of the dynamic workforce at American Airlines. Our 20+ Employee Business Resource Groups are focused on connecting our team members to our customers, suppliers, communities and shareholders, helping team members reach their full potential and creating an inclusive work environment to meet and exceed the needs of our diverse world.
  

  
Are you ready to feel a tremendous sense of pride and satisfaction as you do your part to keep the largest airline in the world running smoothly as we care for people on life’s journey? Feel free to be yourself at American.

title: Grid Systems Integration Architect – Enterprise Systems Integration – 1898 & Co.
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
**Description**
  
 
  

  
 
  
At 1898 & Co., part of Burns & McDonnell, our Enterprise Systems Integration team partners with utilities and critical infrastructure organizations to modernize, integrate, and optimize grid operations and technology environments. We design and deliver resilient, scalable, and secure solutions that support reliable operations, digital transformation, and the convergence of operational technology and enterprise systems.
  
 
  

  
 
  
1898 & Co. is seeking an experienced Grid Systems Integration Architect / Manager to provide utility grid modernization integration consulting services for electric utility clients. This position will join the Enterprise Systems & Integration team and focus on designing, integrating, and implementing grid operations solutions that enable utility transformation initiatives. The successful candidate will possess strong electric utility business and technology acumen, with expertise in operational technology (OT), operational integration, enterprise integration architecture, and utility operations systems.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This role will provide strategic consulting, technical leadership, integration architecture, solution architecture, and project delivery support for grid modernization programs, technology roadmaps, system integrations, and enterprise transformation initiatives. The position requires strong leadership, collaboration, communication, and client engagement skills, while delivering high-quality solutions aligned with utility business objectives.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This position focuses on grid systems architecture, integration, implementation, and optimization of utility operational systems supporting transmission, distribution, outage management, and distributed energy resource programs. A key focus of this role is leading system integration and implementation services for advanced technology solutions, including but not limited to Energy Management Systems (EMS), Outage Management Systems (OMS), Distribution Management Systems (DMS), Advanced Distribution Management Systems (ADMS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), Geographic Information Systems (GIS), Distributed Energy Resource Management Systems (DERMS), and Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI).
  
 
  

  
 
  
The role will lead assessments, technology strategy development, integration architecture design, software implementation efforts, and enterprise transformation initiatives for electric utility clients. The Grid Systems Integration Architect / Manager will work closely with utility executives, business stakeholders, technical teams, vendors, and project managers to develop and deliver integrated solutions that support operational excellence, grid reliability, and future-state modernization strategies.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**What You’ll Do**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Lead the architecture, integration, and implementation of utility operational systems including SCADA, EMS, OMS, DMS, ADMS, DERMS, AMI, and GIS platforms.
  
 
  
+ Develop integration architectures connecting operational technology and enterprise systems using APIs, middleware, messaging frameworks, and cloud-based platforms.
  
 
  
+ Assess current-state environments and define future-state technology roadmaps aligned with grid modernization objectives.
  
 
  
+ Lead requirements gathering activities to define functional, integration, security, performance, and data architecture needs.
  
 
  
+ Design end-to-end integration solutions and data flows supporting interoperability across operational and enterprise systems.
  
 
  
+ Support full system implementation lifecycle including design, configuration, testing, deployment, and production support.
  
 
  
+ Provide technical leadership for system upgrades, platform migrations, and cloud transformation initiatives.
  
 
  
+ Support commissioning, validation, and operational readiness for advanced grid applications such as FLISR, VVO, DER dispatch, and distribution automation.
  
 
  
+ Serve as a trusted advisor to client stakeholders by developing executive-level assessments, business cases, and modernization strategies.
  
 
  
+ Develop and maintain integration artifacts including interface inventories, system interface definitions, and message specifications, ensuring clear documentation of data exchanges, dependencies, and system interactions.
  
 
  
+ Define and document business event models, integration patterns, and message flows to support reliable, scalable interoperability across utility operational and enterprise systems.
  
 
  
+ Lead or support project execution using Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery methodologies, contributing to planning, estimation, scheduling, and coordination of integration-focused workstreams.
  
 
  
+ Support defect triage, issue resolution, and root cause analysis during implementation and testing phases, working closely with cross-functional teams to address system integration challenges.
  
 
  
+ Coordinate and participate in system integration testing activities, including SIT and UAT, while supporting operational readiness efforts such as commissioning, cutover planning, and post-go-live stabilization.
  
 
  
+ Collaborate with project managers, technical teams, and vendors to ensure successful delivery of integrated solutions.
  
 
  
+ Validate architecture and solutions to ensure alignment with enterprise standards, cybersecurity requirements, and operational goals.
  
 
  
+ Develop executive presentations, status reporting, and governance materials to support program decision-making.
  
 
  
+ Mentor junior team members and contribute to continuous improvement and innovation across engagements.
  
 
  
+ Support business development efforts including pursuits, proposal development, and client engagement activities.
  
 
  
+ Perform other duties as assigned.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Preferred Experience & Skills**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Experience integrating utility operational systems such as SCADA, EMS, OMS, DMS, ADMS, DERMS, AMI, and GIS platforms.
  
 
  
+ Strong knowledge of enterprise integration architecture including APIs, middleware platforms, messaging frameworks, and cloud integration patterns.
  
 
  
+ Understanding of electric utility operations including transmission, distribution, outage management, and distributed energy resource integration.
  
 
  
+ Experience supporting large-scale grid modernization or digital transformation initiatives.
  
 
  
+ Proven ability to lead technical teams and workstreams in a client-facing consulting environment.
  
 
  
+ Experience developing executive-level strategy, technology roadmaps, and investment recommendations.
  
 
  
+ Strong communication, facilitation, and stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence executive decision-making.
  
 
  
+ Familiarity with integration platforms such as MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure Integration Services, Kafka, or similar technologies.
  
 
  
+ Experience with major utility system vendors such as GE, Siemens, Schneider Electric, Oracle, or similar platforms.
  
 
  
+ Knowledge of utility cybersecurity frameworks and OT security practices including NERC CIP.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a closely related field from an accredited program, and 7 years of relevant experience required.
  
 
  
+ Architecture and integration skills, including areas such as data architecture, cloud architecture, middleware, communications, or other architectural methods and practices.
  
 
  
+ Understanding of current and emerging associated technologies to manage and deliver quality enterprise solutions in both Information Technology (IT) and Operations Technology (OT) domains.
  
 
  
+ Experience with a variety of project execution methodologies.
  
 
  
+ Experience with leading-edge application(s), operations technology and solutions utilizing various industry standard tools.
  
 
  
+ Experience developing executive-level technology assessments, roadmap strategies focusing on enterprise and/or operational technologies.
  
 
  
+ Strong ability to influence or lead project execution.
  
 
  
+ Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  
 
  
+ Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

title: Consumer Banking Relationship Specialist (Relationship Banker)
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  

  
 Responsible for utilizing knowledge of banking products and services to build professional relationships with customers and engaging in meaningful conversations regarding their financial needs and goals. Promote a positive, helpful, and friendly team environment and provide exceptional customer service at all times 
  

  

  
 
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  
+ Cultivate professional relationships with customers, utilizing assessment tools and engaging customers in meaningful conversations to uncover needs, make appropriate recommendations, and submit referrals to banking center team members or line of business partners.
  
+ Utilize assessment tools to maintain detailed notes regarding customer conversations and interactions.
  
+ Open and service deposit accounts.
  
+ Facilitate and process account transactions accurately and efficiently to minimize errors and reduce fraud.
  
+ Actively participate in morning huddles, calling efforts, business development, and constructive coaching conversations with leadership.
  
+ Demonstrate empathy and proactively resolve client concerns in a timely, professional, and positive manner, escalating issues to next level of authority, as needed.
  
+ Collaborate effectively with line of business partners to foster teamwork, exceptional customer service, and continuous learning.
  
+ Actively promote teamwork, leading by example and taking initiative to assist others.
  
+ Provide backup and assistance to other retail banking roles and locations, as necessary.
  
+ Model and champion the Bank’s standards for exceptional customer service.
  
+ Enthusiastically support the bank’s values and mission.
  
+ Display a high degree of integrity, trustworthiness, and professionalism at all times.
  
+ Complete all essential training timely.
  
+ Display enthusiasm for continuous learning, accepting and applying constructive feedback from more experienced team members.
  
+ Maintain consistently good punctuality and attendance to work.
  
+ Adhere to all Bank policies, procedures, and guidelines. 
  

  

  

  

  
 
  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  
+ Knowledge of bank products and services (e.g., online banking, mobile banking applications, banking cards)
  
+ Knowledge of bank policies and procedures
  
+ Critical thinking and communication skills
  
+ Ability to provide excellent customer service and demonstrate a helpful, friendly, approachable, enthusiastic, and professional disposition
  
+ Ability to demonstrate confidence and act as a trusted advisor
  
+ Ability to communicate effectively both verbally and in writing
  
+ Ability to demonstrate team player approach, capable of thriving in a continually changing environment
  
+ Ability to demonstrate critical thinking skills and accurate and efficient productivity, including attention to detail
  
+ Ability to work without close supervision
  
+ Ability to maintain confidentiality
  
+ Ability to follow policy and procedure including safety and security procedures
  
+ Ability to travel to other work locations (e.g., training, staffing shortages), as needed
  
+ Skill in using computer and Microsoft Office applications necessary to perform essential job functions 
  

  

  

  

  
 
  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  
+ High school diploma or equivalent required; bachelor’s degree preferred
  
+ 1+ year experience interacting with people and displaying excellent service skills, demonstrated through work, military and/or education, required
  
+ Basic knowledge of personal finance and household budgeting required
  
+ Knowledge of, experience with, and ability to explain bank products, online banking, mobile banking applications, banking cards, etc. preferred

Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation.

title: Sr. L&D Specialist – Instructional Designer
description_text:
About the Role:
  
As a CBRE Sr. Learning and Development Specialist - Instructional Designer, you will partner closely with business units to assess learner needs and recommend scalable, sustainable trainings solutions that support business priorities. This role is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing high-quality learning programs using a mix of instructor-led, virtual, digital and self-directed learning approaches. The role applies instructional design methodologies, learning technologies, and microlearning strategies to create engaging and scalable learning solutions that support business outcomes.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning needs and make recommendations for appropriate solutions that meet business demands.
  
· Designs and administers blended training programs and digital learning resources including eLearnings, videos and microlearnings and performance support materials using instructional design principles. Coordinates with stakeholders to ensure effective outcomes.
  
· Applies instructional design principles and adult learning theory to create engaging, effective and scalable learning experiences.
  
· Evaluates and leverages learning technologies and emerging digital tools to enhance content development, learner engagement, and the delivery of innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and supports the delivery of virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Applies instructional design principles to translate complex concepts into clear, practical learning experiences tailored to the needs of diverse audiences and job roles.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops sound relationships with business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
· Solicit and incorporate feedback from participants, peers, and leaders to strengthen learning solutions and professional practice.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 2-5 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience applying instructional design methodologies and adult learning principles.
  
· Experience designing digital learning content including eLearning, videos, microlearning and blended learning solutions.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Excellent organization and project skills to coordinate program efforts.
  
· Ability to analyze possible solutions using technical experience to apply appropriate judgment and precedents.
  
· In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
· Experience with learning management systems (LMS), such as Cornerstone OnDemand (CSOD).
  
· Experience with utilizing multimedia authoring tools such as Adobe Captivate, Articulate Storyline, Rise 360, Vyond and other emerging technologies to design online content.
  
CBRE carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. The minimum salary for the  Sr. Learning & Development Specialist-Instructional Designer position is $80,000 annually [or $38.46 per hour] and the maximum salary for the  Sr. Learning & Development Specialist-Instructional Designer position is $90,000 annually [or $43.26 per hour]. The compensation that is offered to a successful candidate will depend on the candidate’s skills, qualifications, and experience.
  
Equal Employment Opportunity: CBRE is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We have a long-standing commitment to providing equal employment opportunity to all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status, political belief, or any other basis protected by applicable law.
  
Candidate Accommodations: CBRE values the differences of all current and prospective employees and recognizes how every employee contributes to our company’s success. CBRE provides reasonable accommodations in job application procedures for individuals with disabilities. If you require assistance due to a disability in the application or recruitment process, please submit a request via email at recruitingaccommodations@cbre.com or via telephone at +1 866 225 3099 (U.S.) and +1 866 388 4346 (Canada).

CBRE, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer (Women/Minorities/Persons with Disabilities/US Veterans)

title: GIS Analyst (BLR)
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Experience working with utility data, preferably Electric, Gas or Water utility data.
  
 
  
+ Experience performing vector data management and data creation/curation activities in ArcGIS.
  
 
  
+ Experience with GIS data management using an SDE (ArcGIS Software) and/or experience of working with an RDBMS (Relational database management system). Both skills are bonus.
  
 
  
+ Experience of working with ArcGIS Utility Network is a bonus.
  
 
  
+ Attention to detail and an ability to follow process and procedure.
  
 
  
+ Experience working with Enterprise Asset Management software like Trimble Unity/Cityworks, SAP, Maximo, etc. Or working on integration between these systems and GIS is a bonus.
  
 
  
+ Experience performing business analyst functions on geospatial projects, determining requirements and testing is a bonus.
  
 
  
+ Experience of working with software like FME Desktop/Server or any other open-source GIS software is a bonus.
  
 
  
+ Programming knowledge or work experience in languages like Python, JavaScript, SQL or C# is a bonus.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
